commands.review_summary:
The PR moves Telegram command deletion into the empty-menu sync path so non-empty command-menu updates call setMyCommands directly, with colocated tests for non-empty, empty, and hash-cache behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows non-empty sync still deletes both Telegram command scopes before setMyCommands, and the focused test asserts that old order; I did not run live Telegram in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor action is needed for live Telegram proof and a branch refresh; automation cannot provide the contributor's real setup evidence for this external PR.

target.title: fix(telegram): skip delete before non-empty command sync
target.body:
## Summary
- skip `deleteMyCommands()` before syncing a non-empty Telegram command menu
- preserve explicit deletes when syncing an empty menu
- add targeted tests for both paths and stabilize the unchanged-hash test

## Why
PR #32059 already avoids unnecessary syncs when the menu hash is unchanged. When the command set changes, startup still clears Telegram commands before setting the new non-empty menu. This follow-up keeps the explicit clear behavior for empty menus while using a single `setMyCommands()` call for the normal non-empty path.

## Testing
- `pnpm -C /Users/pingu/.openclaw/tmp/openclaw-pr-shallow exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.test.ts`
target.author: p3nchan
